title: AEGIS RAG Security Lab
summary: Secure RAG architecture lab focused on keyword retrieval, semantic retrieval, local GraphRAG, prompt injection risk, threat modeling, governance, monitoring, and adversarial evaluation.

logs.title: Completed Phase 02 threat modeling
logs.details: Completed the initial AI security threat model for the RAG lab after implementing keyword, semantic, and GraphRAG retrieval paths. The phase defined assets, actors, trust boundaries, retrieval-specific attack surfaces, attack paths, and control gaps before adversarial test cases or defensive controls are added.

logs.title: Merged Phase 01 and Phase 01.5 retrieval pipeline
logs.details: Integrated the baseline keyword RAG pipeline and Phase 01.5 semantic retrieval path into main, including local document ingestion, chunking, keyword retrieval, deterministic local embeddings, in-memory vector retrieval, comparison CLI tooling, pytest coverage, and phase documentation.

logs.title: Documented planned Phase 01.75 GraphRAG architecture
logs.details: Outlined the next retrieval-architecture expansion: a local, deterministic GraphRAG path for comparing keyword retrieval, semantic retrieval, and relationship-aware retrieval before formal threat modeling begins.

logs.title: Implemented and merged Phase 01.75 local GraphRAG retrieval
logs.details: Built a local GraphRAG layer with graph nodes, graph edges, concept aliases, deterministic graph construction, traversal-based retrieval, a GraphRAG pipeline, CLI runners, all-mode retrieval comparison, pytest coverage, and phase documentation. Merged the completed phase into main while preserving the phase branch as an architectural checkpoint.

logs.title: Validated GraphRAG behavior locally
logs.details: Ran the full pytest suite successfully and exercised the GraphRAG CLI against a retrieval/confidential-source-material query. The output confirmed three loaded documents, three indexed chunks, twelve graph nodes, eleven graph edges, and explainable graph retrieval details.

nextSteps: Review the Phase 02 documentation state after the connector truncation/debugging work
Convert threat-model findings into adversarial retrieval test cases
Define retrieval-layer controls for source trust, context handling, and graph relationship abuse
Keep implemented controls separate from planned controls in public documentation
